jd.title: Senior Avionics Maintenance Engineer
jd.text:
Senior Avionics Maintenance Engineer
Saudia Aerospace Engineering Industries (SAEI)
Jeddah, Saudi Arabia

About SAEI:
SAEI is a leading MRO provider in the Middle East, serving commercial and government aviation clients. We maintain and overhaul aircraft for Saudi Arabian Airlines and external customers.

Role Summary:
We are seeking an experienced Senior Avionics Maintenance Engineer to lead avionics maintenance operations in our wide-body hangar facility. The role involves hands-on technical leadership, team supervision, and ensuring compliance with GACA, FAA, and EASA regulations.

Key Responsibilities:
- Lead avionics maintenance teams (20-30 technicians) across C-check and D-check programs
- Supervise fault diagnosis, troubleshooting, and rectification of avionics systems (navigation, communication, radar, FMS)
- Ensure compliance with GACA CAR Part-145, FAA, and manufacturer maintenance manuals
- Manage Airworthiness Directives (ADs) and Service Bulletin (SB) compliance tracking
- Review and approve work packages, task cards, and engineering orders
- Coordinate with OEMs (Boeing, Airbus) for technical support and warranty claims
- Drive reliability improvement programs using MTBF/MTTR data analysis
- Manage maintenance planning and scheduling using SAP/AMOS
- Conduct root cause analysis for repetitive defects
- Support CAMO activities and audit preparation
- Mentor junior engineers and manage OJT programs

Required Qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in Avionics, Electrical, or Aerospace Engineering
- 10+ years of experience in aircraft avionics maintenance
- GACA or FAA Aircraft Maintenance Engineer (AME) license preferred
- Experience with wide-body aircraft (B777, B787, A330, A350)
- Proficiency in maintenance management systems (SAP, AMOS, TRAX)
- Strong knowledge of ATA chapters 22-34 (avionics systems)
- Experience with Part-145 approved maintenance organization
- Excellent leadership and team management skills

Preferred:
- PMP or project management certification
- Experience with military aircraft transition to commercial MRO
- Arabic language skills
- EASA Part-66 B2 license

Benefits:
- Competitive salary + housing allowance
- Annual leave + ticket
- Medical insurance for family
- Professional development support

key_matches: AEW&C maintenance: SAAB 2000 fleet of 80 technicians (exceeds 20-30 team size)
Airworthiness Directives and Service Bulletins (directly mentioned)
gaps_to_bridge: Military aircraft, not commercial wide-body (B777, etc.) — bridge: 'avionics principles transfer, platform training readily acquired'
No GACA/FAA AME license — bridge: 'military equivalent maintenance authority, eligible for conversion'
No SAP experience (has AMOS) — bridge: 'AMOS proficiency, SAP-learnable'
No ATA chapter-specific experience listed — bridge: 'military avionics covers equivalent system domains'
expected_output_notes.position_assessment: Competitive — strong technical match with maintenance leadership, but military-to-commercial gap requires bridging
cv_sections_to_emphasize: AEW&C maintenance role (Role #3) — most relevant, team of 80, AMOS, AD/SB compliance
Crotale field role (Role #6) — field operations leadership, readiness management
PAC Kamra (Role #1) — modification programs, configuration management
